records.ety: [OF. espier, F. pier, from OHG. speh<?/n to watch, spy, G. spähen; akin to L. specere to look, species sight, shape, appearance, kind. See Spice, Spy, and cf. Espionage.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Espied (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Espying.]

defs: To catch sight of; to perceive with the eyes; to discover, as a distant object partly concealed, or not obvious to notice; to see at a glance; to discern unexpectedly; to spy; as, to espy land; to espy a man in a crowd.
quotes: As one of them opened his sack to give his ass provender in the inn, . . . he espied his money.
A goodly vessel did I then espy Come like a giant from a haven broad.

defs: To inspect narrowly; to examine and keep watch upon; to watch; to observe.
quotes: He sends angels to espy us in all our ways.

defs: To look or search narrowly; to look about; to watch; to take notice; to spy.
quotes: Stand by the way, and espy.
